entries.title:
About Learners engage with microscopes, airplanes, and sound as they practice beginning lab skills and learn to construct bar graphs from their data. The lab component is required for this course. Coordinating afternoon activities are provided in Outdoor Work.

This topic is included in the following course(s): Science: Grade 4

About Science: Grade 4
In level 4, learners begin to notice science in their community and begin to develop their own interests as they discover a variety of topics from the microscopic world, physical science, and more. A mixture of more and less challenging books is included in this course.

entries.content:
Science: Grade 4
The Big Picture:
To accomplish the goal of supporting a relationship with the Things of the Universe

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: a Mason science program consists of nature lore

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: natural history

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: and general science. Nature immersion

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: inquiry

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: community connection

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: and supportive literature are woven into each of these three parts. Building on the familiarity and foundational knowledge of Form 1

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: Form 2 learners in grades 4-6 extend the scope of the Things they encounter and begin to notice that science is active in their world. Growing in their self-knowledge at this age

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: they may notice that they gravitate toward some of these Things more than others. This is a bit like noticing that you have a natural rapport with a particular friend because they seem to understand the way you think and share the same interests. This is wonderful and very typical

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: but we still want them to get to know more “friends” in science and try different Things

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: even though one might be their particular interest. Their sense of community is expanding

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: as well: who are the scientists in their world

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: and what are they doing? Special care has been taken to build vocabulary and conceptual knowledge “by the way” and to maintain the connection between scientific endeavors and the human experience. In Form 3

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content:
students will continue to build on this knowledge of what science is doing in the world when they examine science in its historical context.

Nature lore is timeless knowledge that is passed through a community

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: much like a grandmother passes on how to make that special bread when the dough just "feels right." Like Mason

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: we strive to pass on this knowledge primarily through outdoor work. Group nature walks

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: seasonal readings

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: and topics in scouting are provided as an Outdoor Work resource in the Quick Links. If desired

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: literature suggestions to support lore can be found in the Community Read Alouds resource (in Citizenship Grades 4+).

entries.content:
About Science: Grade 4
In level 4, learners begin to notice science in their community and begin to develop their own interests as they discover a variety of topics from the microscopic world, physical science, and more. A mixture of more and less challenging books is included in this course.

General Science: Grade 4
For fourth-grade students or possibly hungry third-graders or fifth-graders taking their time.  If combining grades is desirable: the combined grades may begin at the appropriate level and move forward together, or teachers can adapt the reading level and laboratory content to meet their needs.
